title: [InstCombine] Relax one-use requirement for add iN (sext i1 X), (sext i1 Y) --> sext (X | Y) to iN
summary:
The generalization relaxed the one-use requirement from both sext operands to just one sext having a single use. This allows the optimization to fire when the other sext has extra uses (like stores or calls), as shown in the new test cases. The key insight is that the transformation still reduces instruction count by removing the add and one sext, even if the other sext remains live elsewhere.

**Actionable takeaways:**
1. When generalizing pattern matches, analyze which operands truly need one-use constraints versus which can have multiple uses.
2. Consider whether the transformation reduces total instructions even with extra uses—if so, relax constraints incrementally.
3. Add test cases that stress the relaxed constraints (e.g., extra uses in different positions) to ensure robustness.
